questions.statement: ______ are specially designed computers that perform complex calculations extremely rapidly.
India&#39;s only active volcano is located in ______.
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Andaman and Nicobar islands.

Key Points

  • Barren Island, located in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, is the site of India's only active volcano.
  • This volcano is situated approximately 140 km northeast of Port Blair, the capital of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
  • The volcano erupted in 1991 after being dormant for nearly 150 years, and since then, it has continued to show intermittent activity.
  • Barren Island is uninhabited due to its volcanic nature and is primarily visited by researchers and scientists.
  • The island is part of the volcanic belt that runs from Sumatra in Indonesia to Myanmar and is one of the few active volcanoes in the Indian subcontinent.

Additional Information

  • Formation of Barren Island:
    • Barren Island is part of the Indian tectonic plate that interacts with the Indo-Australian plate, leading to volcanic activity.
    • It is classified as a stratovolcano, characterized by layers of hardened lava, tephra, and volcanic ash.
    • The island's formation is linked to the movement of tectonic plates and subduction zones in the region.
  • Features of Barren Island:
    • The island spans an area of approximately 3 km in diameter.
    • The central cone of the volcano rises about 350 meters above sea level.
    • The island is surrounded by volcanic rocks and black sand beaches, which are evidence of past eruptions.
  • Volcanic Activity:
    • Barren Island has experienced multiple eruptions, with notable ones recorded in 2017, 2008, and 1994.
    • The eruptions have resulted in lava flows and ash clouds, impacting the surrounding marine ecosystem.
  • Scientific Importance:
    • The island serves as a site for volcanology research, helping scientists study the dynamics of active volcanoes.
    • Research conducted here contributes to the understanding of plate tectonics and volcanic processes in the region.

Important Points

  • Other Geological Features:
    • The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are part of the volcanic arc system known as the Sunda Arc.
    • They are located along the boundary of the Indo-Australian plate and the Eurasian plate, making the region seismically and volcanically active.
  • Tourist Restrictions:
    • Access to Barren Island is restricted, and visitors are not allowed to set foot on the island due to safety concerns.
    • Tourists can view the island and its volcanic activity from boats or ships at a safe distance.
  • Global Context:
    • Barren Island is one of the few active volcanoes in the region, alongside others in Indonesia and the Pacific Ring of Fire.
    • Its activity provides insights into the broader volcanic and tectonic dynamics of Southeast Asia.
